name: Bertolt Brecht

acts.description: Systematically appropriated work of female collaborators including Elisabeth Hauptmann without credit or pay
Maintained multiple simultaneous relationships with women collaborators who he exploited professionally and personally

behavior_summary: The great left-wing playwright built his career substantially on uncredited work by women collaborators he was also sleeping with. Threepenny Opera may be partly Elisabeth Hauptmann's.

art.summary: Invented epic theater. The most influential playwright of the 20th century after possibly Shakespeare. His Verfremdungseffekt shaped all subsequent political theater.

verdict.blurb: He wrote plays about exploitation and capitalist theft while running a personal workshop of women whose labor and love he extracted without credit. Elisabeth Hauptmann wrote 80% of Threepenny Opera by some accounts. Her name was footnote material for decades.
